calendar.matters:
REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (835 Excelsior Avenue. San Francisco, Ca 94112)
Off Calendar Jul-30-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Petitioner

The sale was confirmed at $1,163,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed on June 21, 2018. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required. Ms. Ng to submit proposed order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For The Appointment Of Successor Trustee Due To Death Of Prior Trustee (Prob. C. ; 15643(D), And 15660[D])
Off Calendar Feb-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (trustee) Vera Sprinkel. Matthew Cebrian for May Wong. Jeffrey Jaech (via court call) for Darrell Wong.

The Court stated that this is a petition for a settlement agreement along with 2 other petitions that will go off calendar. Ms. Ng stated that the signature has been filed and that she expected this to be a pre-grant. The Court confirmed that there are no objections to granting the settlement agreement. Mr. Cebrian stated that the other petitions were dealt with in the settlement agreement and Mr. Jaech confirmed that they could go off calendar. The Court granted the petition for order approving settlement agreement, etc., (subject to review of the order). The Accounting petition and the Petition for Suspension and Removal of Trustee, etc. are off calendar.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement, Authorizing And Instructing Temporary Trustee To Execute Agreement. And Releasing Temporary Trustee From Liability For Selection Of Tax Counsel To Represent The Trust Estate
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Continued To Dec-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Continued To Dec-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (temporary successor trustee) Vera Sprinkel. Jeffrey Jaech (via court call) for Darrell Wong.

The Court stated that no one is present on behalf of May Wong, that pursuant to the settlement agreement Mr. Wong's petition will come off calendar and he will withdraw his objections to Ms. Wong's accounting, that the settlement agreement exempts the temporary trustee from liability regarding tax counsel, that it seems strange to say that the trustee has no obligations regarding this important function but there are no objections, that there are a number of problems with the accounting and at most the Court could only acknowledge it. Ms. Ng stated that it is part of the settlement agreement that Ms. Sprinkel have no liability regarding taxes and that they have provided potential names to Ms. Wong. Mr. Jaech stated that they had not addressed the withdrawal of the accounting petition, that they assume Ms. Wong will take it off calendar, that the petitions can be continued pending dismissal being filed. Ms. Ng stated that they are still waiting for the tax returns, that the provision for the sale of real property does not stated a need for court confirmation, that the parties can come back if there is a problem and that there is not a need for court supervision. Mr. Jaech agreed with Ms. Ng, stated that there is a complication of Ms. Wong not signing the amendment to the settlement agreement but that her attorney signed it. Ms. Ng stated that it is difficult to get signatures from Ms. Wong, that she is getting no response and that she had told Ms. Wong's counsel to be here today. Mr. Jaech suggested that the Court could approve the settlement agreement but not the amendment or that Ms. Wong not be held to it without a signature, that they have a mediation agreement in place, that they can file a petition to enforce the settlement agreement and that the amendment provides an additional $45,000.00 to Ms. Wong. The Court stated that it will continue all of the petitions for clarification from Ms. Wong and her counsel or that the parties can file an amended petition for approval without the amendment. The accounting petition, Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement and the petition for Suspension and Removal of Trustee were continued to December 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the parties were instructed to talk to each other and to either have Ms. Wong sign the amendment or file an amendment.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement, Authorizing And Instructing Temporary Trustee To Execute Agreement. And Releasing Temporary Trustee From Liability For Selection Of Tax Counsel To Represent The Trust Estate
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Continued To Dec-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Continued To Nov-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Jaech (via Court Call) for Darrell Wong. Lillian Ng for (temporary trustee) Vera Sprinkel.

The Court asked for the status on the mediation. Ms. Ng reported that mediation was successful. Mr. Jaech stated that they have settled, that they just need the signatures on the final settlement, that Ms. Ng will file a petition for approval of the settlement and once confirmed the other petitions can go off calendar. Ms. Ng stated that the petition is almost ready to be filed. The Accounting petition and the Petition for Suspension and Removal of Trustee, etc., were continued to November 15, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Continued To Nov-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (temporary trustee) Vera Sprinkel. Jeffrey Jaech (via court call) for Darrell Wong.

The Court stated that it understands that the parties were involved in mediation and asked what the next steps are. Ms. Ng reported that there are special duties that the temporary trustee was asked to perform, that they have provided a status to the parties, that there is lack of cooperation from the prior trustee, that the California Street property is under management, that they are assessing for repairs, that no estate tax has been filed, that there is either a lack of records or a lack of providing records, that they do have some records, that they just received a list from the accountant indicating what she needs, that the referee has a preliminary appraisal but they need a date of death appraisal, that one of the tenants is the former trustee's son or nephew who is away until mid June, that they do not have access to the property, that it has not been an easy route, that they will need to provide the accountants list to the former trustee in the hopes that she has the documents, that one of the properties was sold after death but prior to the trust proceedings and that they hope to have solid information by September regarding the tax liabilities. Mr. Jaech stated that they cannot mediate without the tax liability and values of properties, that they are still interested in mediation, that it is a slow process and suggested setting a trial date. The Court stated that it will not set a trial date when the matter is not even remotely ready to try and that it can only encourage cooperation. Mr. Jaech stated that they cannot do anything until the appraisal and tax returns are done. The Court stated that it encourages the temporary trustee to think about formal steps if she is not getting cooperation, that if they can get mediation done great and if not it will want to know the next steps. The accounting petition and the petition for suspension and removal of trustee, etc., were continued to September 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar May-25-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Jaech (via court call) for Darrell Wong. Matthew Cebrian for (trustee) May Wong.

Mr. Cebrian reported that they have reached an agreement on a limited appointment of a temporary trustee and that they would like to continue to mediate. The Court confirmed that the temporary trustee was Vera Sprinkel and asked how long the temporary appointment would last. Mr. Cebrian stated that the appointment was for 120 days to be able to continue the mediate the other issues. Mr. Jaech stated that the appointment would be indefinite and that they have agreed on specific duties. The Court asked if there was a structure in place for mediation. Mr. Jaech stated that they have agreed to mediation but that they need to get the taxes done first and that they would like a 3 month continuance. The accounting petition and the petition for suspension and removal of trustee, for appointment of temporary trustee, etc., were continued to May 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with Vera Sprinkel being appointed to serve as temporary trustee and the parties to mediate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Bifurcated For The Appointment Of The Temporary Trustee
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Continued To May-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Continued To Feb-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Continued To Feb-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Continued To Feb-10-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Nov-24-2015 Continued To Jan-12-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Cebrian for (trustee) May Wong. Jeffrey Jaech (via court call) for Darrell Wong.

The Court asked where the accounting was. Mr. Cebrian reported that it is not finished yet, that the accountant just received the last documents, that opposing counsel has been apprised of the situation, that they are ready to talk resolution pending the outcome of the accounting, requested a 45 day continuance and that the trust primarily consists of real estate along with 2 accounts. Mr. Jaech stated that they are in opposition, that the beneficiaries have been asking for an accounting for over a year, that the court ordered an accounting at the last hearing, that it is his understanding that the accountant has not received the bank statements, that they propose that the trustee be suspended, that they have a professional fiduciary (Vera Sprinkle) ready to take over, that they do not know what is in the trust, that they do not know what happened to the proceeds of the sale and that taxes are not being paid. The Court clarified that it had not ordered an accounting at the last hearing, that it is concerned that an accounting has not been completed, that it is not prepared to suspend the trustee and is now making an order that the accounting be prepared and filed by January 5, 2016. The Court further stated that it hopes the parties can work this out, that nothing will be known until the accounting is filed which they are now under order to file and that the Court will now be considering suspension of the trustee. Mr. Jaech is to prepare and submit a proposed order once it has been reviewed by Mr. Cebrian as to form. The petition for suspension and removal of trustee, etc. was continued to January 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-26-2015 Continued To Nov-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Suspension And Removal Of Trustee, For Appointment Of Temp. Trustee & Successor Trustee, And For Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-06-2015 Continued To Nov-10-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeffrey Jaech (via court call) for (petitioner) Darrell Wong. Matthew Cebrian for the Trustee.

Mr. Cebrian stated that they are in the process of preparing an accounting, that they need an additional 30 days and that they are planning to petition the court for approval. The Court asked Mr. Jaech is there was any reason not to wait for the accounting before proceeding. Mr. Jaech reported that it has been 7 years since the settlor died, that they were supposed to distribute at that time and that the nephews have been trying to get information for over a year. The Court stated that they are about to get that information, why not wait. Mr. Jaech further reported that property taxes have not bee paid in 5 years, that there are concerns regarding the other apartment building and that they have not faith that an accounting will be filed. Mr. Cebrian stated that there have been issues of obtaining documents, that the non petitioning beneficiary has been acting as trustee and taking funds to update property, that they are trying to unravel where everything went and are trying to get bank statements. The Court asked about the time frame that the accounting covers. Mr. Cebrian stated 4 years. The Court stated that it is without prejudice that that is the correct time period. The petition for suspension and removal of trustee, for appointment of temporary trustee and successor trustee and for accounting was continued to November 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered that the accounting be prepared and submitted to the court in that time frame, that all other petitions are continued to that date, that they are to talk to each other and that if Mr. Jaech wants he can submit a form of order that orders the accounting to be submitted and that the court would need it by November 5, 2015.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)
